positions.detail: Down -69% — stop loss triggered at -50%. Close position.

agent_analysis:
## CRITICAL — Friday Expiry Day

### Position Status

**AMD CALL: DEAD.** -69%, stop was triggered at -50% and should have been closed then. This is a breach of risk protocol. The +1.5% stock move couldn't save it — charm and theta destroyed whatever delta remained. Option is near-worthless. **Close immediately at market.**

**MRVL CALL: DEAD.** -46%, stock is -0.4% against thesis on expiry day. With hours left, delta is likely sub-0.10 (delta stop territory). There is no realistic recovery path — the stock would need a massive move in hours to overcome the theta that's already been paid. **Close immediately at market.**

**RTX PUT: DEAD.** -47%, stock only -0.4% — thesis was right on direction but magnitude was nowhere close to what a weekly put needed. Same as MRVL: delta is collapsing toward zero, theta is in terminal acceleration. Holding into the afternoon risks the last 3-5% of remaining value evaporating to zero. **Close immediately at market.**

---

### Summary

All three positions failed. 0-for-3 week. Two hit or nearly hit the -50% hard stop; the third breached it. None came close to the Friday 10% profit target at any point today.

---

### RECOMMENDATION

**Close all three positions NOW at market. No exceptions. No hoping.**

AMD should have been closed when the stop triggered — execute that immediately. MRVL and RTX are 3-4 points from stop and decaying by the minute on expiry day. Waiting gains nothing; every minute costs premium.

Bid-ask spreads are widening as we approach the afternoon. Get out before the 1:00 PM liquidity cliff makes fills worse. Do not hold past 2:00 PM under any circumstances per methodology rules.

This is a max-loss week. The reflection engine needs to examine whether the directional signals were wrong, or whether strike selection and IV entry were the real culprits — a +1.5% stock move on AMD producing a -69% option loss points to overpaying for premium or targeting too far OTM.
